assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message anchors to a specific signal from the card.
criteria.evidence_quote: Elena, audit season always brings pressure on documentation. Mellow is a CoR with a full trail on each contractor: documents, KYC, payouts — all structured. We remove manual reconciliation and give you a scheme that's easier to explain to leadership and auditors. What's the weakest spot?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.
criteria.evidence_quote: Elena, audit season always brings pressure on documentation. Mellow is a CoR with a full trail on each contractor: documents, KYC, payouts — all structured. We remove manual reconciliation and give you a scheme that's easier to explain to leadership and auditors. What's the weakest spot?
criteria.label: Scope & responsibility boundary

criteria.name: Language fit
criteria.short_reason: Language is adapted to the chosen persona.
criteria.evidence_quote: Elena, audit season always brings pressure on documentation. Mellow is a CoR with a full trail on each contractor: documents, KYC, payouts — all structured. We remove manual reconciliation and give you a scheme that's easier to explain to leadership and auditors. What's the weakest spot?
criteria.label: Persona language fit

criteria.name: Handling core objections
criteria.short_reason: At least two key objections addressed substantively.
criteria.evidence_quote: Elena, audit season always brings pressure on documentation. Mellow is a CoR with a full trail on each contractor: documents, KYC, payouts — all structured. We remove manual reconciliation and give you a scheme that's easier to explain to leadership and auditors. What's the weakest spot?

objections.objection: Context noted. I need the boundary: what's exactly in your scope, what's mine — clearly, without declarations.
Send a summary: flow, SLA and what gets removed from manual reconciliation. If the numbers are real — we'll talk.
Sounds right. But the right words aren't an argument. What specifically will be different?
Ok. Send it over — I'll review.

criteria.label: Core objection handling

criteria.name: Next step
criteria.evidence_quote: The current setup stops being defensible in two scenarios: a payment gets stuck with no SLA path — or diligence comes and there's no audit trail. Your current setup has both risks. Mellow removes them specifically.
criteria.label: Clear next step

transcript.text: Elena, audit season always brings pressure on documentation. Mellow is a CoR with a full trail on each contractor: documents, KYC, payouts — all structured. We remove manual reconciliation and give you a scheme that's easier to explain to leadership and auditors. What's the weakest spot?
Context noted. I need the boundary: what's exactly in your scope, what's mine — clearly, without declarations.

The current setup stops being defensible in two scenarios: a payment gets stuck with no SLA path — or diligence comes and there's no audit trail. Your current setup has both risks. Mellow removes them specifically.
Send a summary: flow, SLA and what gets removed from manual reconciliation. If the numbers are real — we'll talk.

On SLA: a named escalation owner, defined response time in the contract, incident trail documented. Not general support — a process with accountability. What else do you need to understand before moving forward?
Sounds right. But the right words aren't an argument. What specifically will be different?
I'll send you a one-pager: flow, SLA, and how the audit trail looks before diligence. If it speaks to your situation — let's do a short call.
Ok. Send it over — I'll review.
Let me send you a brief written summary: payment flow, audit story, and scope boundary. If it's concrete and grounded, can we find 20 minutes this week?
Waiting for the materials. When will you send them?
